evidences.title: NVIDIA Stock Split: Full Breakdown, History, and Investor Insights
evidences.content: #### Short-Term Stock Performance Historically, stock splits tend to generate positive short-term momentum as investor excitement drives increased trading activity. Following NVIDIA's announcement of its 10-for-1 split on June 7, 2024, the stock price experienced an immediate uptick, rising approximately 5% within the first two days of trading. Post-split, the adjusted share price of $120 led to increased buying activity among retail investors, who saw the lower price as a more accessible entry point. Trading volumes also surged as both new and existing investors capitalized on the split-adjusted shares. [...] 2. June 2024: Most recently, NVIDIA announced a 10-for-1 stock split in response to its stock price surpassing $1,000 per share. This split was NVIDIA’s largest to date and underscored its position as a leader in the AI chip market. The move came on the heels of record-breaking earnings, driven by soaring demand for NVIDIA's GPUs, particularly in AI model training and cloud infrastructure. By reducing the share price, NVIDIA aimed to make its stock more accessible to retail investors, enhancing liquidity and expanding its investor base. [...] 1. July 2021: NVIDIA implement...

evidences.title: NVIDIA (NVDA) - Stock split history - Companies Market Cap
evidences.content: # Stock split history for NVIDIA (NVDA) NVIDIA stock (symbol: NVDA) underwent a total of 6 stock splits. The most recent stock split occurred on June 10th, 2024. One NVDA share bought prior to June 27th, 2000 would equal to 480 NVDA shares today. ### Stock split list | Date | Split | Multiple | Cumulative multiple | --- --- | | 2024-06-10 | 10:1 | x10 | x480 | | 2021-07-20 | 4:1 | x4 | x48 | | 2007-09-11 | 3:2 | x1.5 | x12 | | 2006-04-07 | 2:1 | x2 | x8 | | 2001-09-12 | 2:1 | x2 | x4 | | 2000-06-27 | 2:1 | x2 | x2 | ### Stock splits for similar companies or competitors [...] The company was founded in January 1993 by Jen-Hsun Huang, Curtis Priem and Chris Malachowsky. In May 1995, Nvidia launched the NV1 (STG-2000), one of the first 3D accelerator processors (GPU). In January 1999, Nvidia was included in the NASDAQ (NVDA) and delivered the ten millionth graphics chip in the same year. evidences.title: Will Nvidia Announce a Stock Split on Nov. 19? | The Motley Fool
evidences.content: ## An Nvidia stock split announcement is unlikely Over Nvidia's history, it has undergone six total stock splits. The most recent ones occurred in 2021 and 2024, and each was announced following the first-quarter earnings report. The 2024 split was a 10-for-1 split, and Nvidia's share price was a split-adjusted $1,220 at that level. In 2021, Nvidia's stock split at $744. Nvidia Stock Quote ## NASDAQ: NVDA ### Key Data Points Both of those prices are far above where Nvidia currently is, but that's not the only factor to consider. Nvidia is a member of the Dow Jones Industrial Average (^DJI 0.18%), which is a price-weighted index. This means that the companies must stay at about an equal dollar-per-share level. Otherwise, they could throw the whole index out of whack.

evidences.title: NVDA Stock Splits - NVIDIA Corp - Alpha Spread
evidences.content: ## NVDA's latest stock split occurred on Jun 10, 2024 The company executed a 10-for-1 stock split, meaning that for every share held, investors received 10 new shares. Before the split, NVDA traded at 1208.88 per share. Afterward, the share price was about 121.7347. The adjusted shares began trading on Jun 10, 2024. This was NVDA's 7th stock split, following the previous one in Jul 20, 2021. evidences.title: Nvidia stock rises after 10-for-1 stock split - Yahoo Finance
evidences.content: ### [...] The split means that owners of Nvidia common stock held as of the close of market on Thursday received 10 shares for each one share they held. For example, if a shareholder owned four shares of Nvidia as of Thursday, they now own 40 shares post-split. Stock splits make owning shares of a stock more affordable by lowering the price of individual shares without diluting the value of existing shareholders' total holdings. "The stock split is going to make Nvidia a lot more reachable for a lot of these retail traders," Option Research & Technology Services' Matt Amberson told Yahoo Finance last Thursday. "Now, you rarely see a stock over $1,000 with a 50% implied volatility, so the prices of the options are extraordinarily high, so options traders are really looking forward to the split." [...] ### New on Yahoo
